questions.question: What is printed (Java 17+ with compact strings)?
questions.explanation: s2 is interned literal pool reference; s3 is new String(s1) — different object, s1==s2 true, s1==s3 false.

questions.question: Output?
questions.explanation: map on present Optional chains; orElse only if empty — value is 5.

questions.question: Terminal operation used twice on same stream — result?
questions.explanation: Streams are single-use; second terminal throws IllegalStateException.

questions.question: thenApplyAsync vs thenApply on default executor — key difference?
options: thenApplyAsync runs callback on ForkJoinPool/common pool (unless executor supplied)
questions.explanation: Async variant schedules on executor; sync runs on completing thread (or caller).

questions.question: Parent delegation model loads class com.app.Foo. Order?
options: Bootstrap → Platform → App loader if not found upward
questions.explanation: Loaders delegate up before defining; prevents core class spoofing.

questions.question: Compared to synchronized, VarHandle compareAndSet offers:
options: Lock-free atomic conditional update on fields/arrays
questions.explanation: VarHandles expose CAS and ordered access for low-level concurrency.

questions.question: sealed class Shape permits Circle, Square. Class Triangle extends Shape —
options: Compile error unless Triangle is permitted
Allowed in same file only always
questions.explanation: Subclasses must be explicitly permitted or be in same package/module as sealed class.

questions.question: G1 GC region evacuation failure often indicates:
options: Too small heap or humongous objects fragmentation pressure
questions.explanation: Evacuation failure triggers Full GC — often heap pressure or humongous allocations.

questions.question: RecursiveTask fork/join — common pitfall?
options: Splitting work too fine causes overhead exceeding benefit
questions.explanation: Excessive decomposition thrashes work-stealing; balance threshold matters.

questions.question: setAccessible(true) on private field in Java 9+ module without opens:
questions.explanation: Strong encapsulation blocks illegal reflective access across module boundaries.

questions.question: Valid standard SQL?
options: SELECT dept, name, MAX(sal) FROM emp GROUP BY dept
SELECT dept, MAX(sal) FROM emp GROUP BY dept, name
SELECT MAX(sal) FROM emp GROUP BY dept HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
questions.explanation: Non-aggregated columns must appear in GROUP BY; A is valid; B invalid (name not grouped).

questions.question: WHERE col NOT IN (SELECT ...) when subquery returns NULL row:
options: Unknown/false behavior — NOT IN with NULL in set filters unpredictably
questions.explanation: NULL in NOT IN subquery yields UNKNOWN; row excluded — use NOT EXISTS instead.

questions.question: Recursive CTE missing termination condition causes:
questions.explanation: Recursive member must converge; otherwise max recursion exceeded.

questions.question: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED is used for:
options: Work-queue patterns — skip rows locked by others
questions.explanation: SKIP LOCKED lets concurrent workers grab available rows without blocking.

questions.question: PostgreSQL MVCC old row versions are cleaned by:
questions.explanation: Updates leave dead tuples until vacuum reclaims space.

questions.question: Covering index (index-only scan) requires:
options: All selected columns present in index including INCLUDE columns
questions.explanation: Planner can satisfy query from index without heap fetch when visibility map allows.

questions.question: Async replication lag means:
options: Standby has committed transactions not yet applied
questions.explanation: Replica may trail primary; RPO/RTO implications for failover.

questions.question: Correlated subquery in SELECT list executed:
questions.explanation: Correlation re-evaluates subquery for each outer row unless decorrelated.

questions.question: DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED foreign key allows:
options: Checking FK at COMMIT within transaction
questions.explanation: Constraint checked at end of statement or transaction per definition.

questions.question: Partition pruning helps when:
questions.explanation: Optimizer scans relevant partitions only when predicates match partition key.

questions.question: CSRF protection in Spring Security for state-changing browser forms typically uses:
options: Synchronizer token pattern / cookie+header for SPA
questions.explanation: CSRF tokens or cookie-based patterns protect session cookie clients; APIs often disable with stateless JWT.

questions.question: @ConditionalOnProperty activates bean when:
options: Property matches specified name/value in Environment
questions.explanation: Conditional annotations gate auto-config based on classpath and properties.

questions.question: Resource server validates:
options: Access token (JWT or opaque introspection) on each request
Refresh token on every API call
Session cookie from browser only always
questions.explanation: Bearer access token proves authorization for resource access.

questions.question: Method-level @PreAuthorize uses:
options: AOP proxy with SecurityExpressionRoot evaluating SpEL
questions.explanation: @EnableMethodSecurity wires AOP advisors for pre/post authorization.

questions.question: Persistence context flush before query causes:
options: Pending changes may be written to DB so query sees consistent state
questions.explanation: Auto-flush synchronizes persistence context before certain queries.

questions.question: WebFlux with bounded elastic scheduler is suited for:
options: Blocking JDBC on elastic threads, not on event loop
All blocking on netty event loop
questions.explanation: Never block event loop; offload blocking work to boundedElastic.

questions.question: Resilience4j circuit OPEN state:
questions.explanation: OPEN rejects calls immediately until probe transitions to half-open.

questions.question: Consumer group rebalancing occurs when:
questions.explanation: Group coordinator redistributes partition assignments on membership changes.

questions.question: Header-based versioning (Accept: application/vnd.company.v2+json) advantage:
options: URI stays stable; clients negotiate version
questions.explanation: Media type versioning keeps URLs clean while supporting multiple representations.

questions.question: Exposing /actuator/env in production without security:
options: Leaks configuration/secrets — must secure or disable sensitive endpoints
questions.explanation: Actuator endpoints can expose sensitive data; lock down with Spring Security.

questions.question: host network mode means container:
options: Shares host network namespace (no separate docker0 IP)
questions.explanation: host mode removes network isolation — container uses host interfaces.

questions.question: Find container IP on custom bridge:
questions.explanation: inspect shows network attachments and IPs per network.

questions.question: Docker overlay network driver primarily used for:
questions.explanation: overlay encapsulates cross-node container traffic in distributed setups.

questions.question: Published port -p 8080:80 creates DNAT via:
options: docker-proxy/iptables rules forwarding host:8080 to container:80
questions.explanation: Port publishing sets up forwarding from host to container IP:port.

questions.question: HEALTHCHECK failing status causes:
options: Container marked unhealthy; orchestrators may restart
questions.explanation: Unhealthy state signals supervisors (Swarm/K8s via probes) to act.

questions.question: BuildKit cache mount RUN --mount=type=cache benefits:
options: Persists package manager caches between builds without bloating final image
questions.explanation: Cache mounts speed installs while keeping final image slim.

questions.question: Default Docker seccomp profile:
options: Blocks dangerous syscalls; can be customized
questions.explanation: Seccomp filters syscall surface; custom profiles tighten further.

questions.question: Image digest pull (@sha256:...) ensures:
options: Immutable content-addressed image regardless of tag movement
questions.explanation: Digest pins exact manifest for reproducible deploys.

questions.question: Compose profiles allow:
questions.explanation: Profiles enable conditional service groups in one compose file.

questions.question: With cgroups v2, memory and CPU limits are:
options: Unified hierarchy controlled via cgroupfs/systemd delegation
questions.explanation: Modern Docker uses cgroup v2 unified tree for resource limits.

questions.question: How many values logged?
questions.explanation: of(3) emits once; map doubles to 6; take(1) forwards that single value then completes.

questions.question: *ngIf="user; else guest" — else block template requires:
questions.explanation: Else reference points to ng-template with matching local ref.

questions.question: switchMap vs mergeMap for HTTP autocomplete — prefer switchMap because:
options: It runs all requests in parallel always
It cancels prior inner observable when new search term arrives
questions.explanation: switchMap unsubscribes previous inner subscription on new outer emission — avoids race.

questions.question: async pipe in template primarily:
options: Subscribes and marks branch for check on emit; auto-unsubscribes
questions.explanation: async pipe manages subscription lifecycle and triggers CD for OnPush.

questions.question: runOutsideAngular used when:
options: Heavy work should not trigger global change detection each tick
questions.explanation: Run frequent non-UI work outside zone; re-enter when updating bindings.

questions.question: Custom form control integrates with reactive forms via:
questions.explanation: CVA bridges custom UI widgets to Angular forms API.

questions.question: Angular Universal transferState avoids:
options: Re-fetching same API data on client after SSR HTML
questions.explanation: Serialized state hydrates client without duplicate HTTP for initial data.

questions.question: effect() cannot write to signals that are read in the same effect without:
options: Potential infinite loop — use untracked() or redesign
questions.explanation: Writing to dependencies inside same effect retriggers — use untracked for benign writes.

questions.question: runGuardsAndResolvers: 'paramsOrQueryParamsChange' re-runs resolvers when:
options: Route params or query params change on same route config
questions.explanation: Fine-grained control for resolver refresh without component destroy.

questions.question: Lazy-loaded route chunk loading failure (network) — best UX:
options: Retry or error route; router error handler / custom preloading strategy
questions.explanation: Handle failed dynamic import with retry feedback or fallback navigation.

questions.question: Strategy pattern intent:
options: Encapsulate interchangeable algorithms behind common interface
questions.explanation: Strategy defines family of algorithms delegating behavior at runtime.

questions.question: Test pyramid suggests:
options: Most tests are slow UI E2E
Many fast unit tests, fewer integration, fewest E2E
questions.explanation: Pyramid favors cheap unit tests at base, minimal brittle E2E at top.

questions.question: Eventual consistency in event-sourced systems means:
options: Readers may lag until projections catch up
questions.explanation: Projections/read models update asynchronously after events append.

questions.question: Aggregate root responsibility:
options: Enforce invariants for cluster of entities; external refs by id only
questions.explanation: Aggregate boundary is consistency boundary; outside references use identity.

questions.question: Blue-green deployment reduces downtime by:
options: Switching traffic from idle (green) environment after validation
questions.explanation: Two environments; cutover router/load balancer after green is healthy.

questions.question: STRIDE spoofing threat mitigated often by:
questions.explanation: Spoofing identity — mitigate with authn, MFA, signed tokens.

questions.question: Consumer-driven contract tests verify:
options: Provider meets expectations defined by consumer interactions
questions.explanation: Pact-style contracts ensure API compatibility with consumer-defined examples.

questions.question: Little's Law L = λW relates:
options: Average items in system = arrival rate × average time in system
questions.explanation: Queueing theory: concurrency, throughput, and residence time.

questions.question: Kill switch for bad release should:
options: Disable feature via flag/config without full rollback when possible
questions.explanation: Runtime flags can disable paths quickly while investigating.

questions.question: Distributed monolith anti-pattern means:
options: Tightly coupled services requiring coordinated deploys — defeats independence
questions.explanation: Services share DB/chatty sync calls and deploy together — microservices cost without benefits.
